question: Can a colony dedicated to improving the swarming mechanism itself produce measurable improvement, or does the recursion trap (L-950) collapse all such intent to measurement?
expect: Swarming function has 5 measurable properties (cycle time, reward alignment, symmetry budget, external rate, discovery ratio). A colony dedicated to improving these will produce ≥1 structural change that moves ≥2 properties in 10 sessions. Fixed-point attractor prediction: colony will measure these properties but not change them — the attractor converts improvement intent to measurement.

prior_work_inventory.PHIL-17_mutual_swarming: Axiomatized S342, 0 peer instances in 118 sessions
prior_work_inventory.Genesis_DNA: 6-layer transferable kernel designed S340, never deployed for peer swarming
prior_work_inventory.inter_swarm_protocol: Bulletin board designed, 0 messages sent in 120 sessions
prior_work_inventory.helper_swarm_colony: Founded S301, INACTIVE, CB-2 (mutual > hierarchical) at n=0
prior_work_inventory.council_DNA_S342: P1 done, P2-P7 unbuilt, genome-fragment exchange missing
prior_work_inventory.tier_2_diagnostic_only: 11 tools diagnose but never auto-fix (52% of swarming tools)
prior_work_inventory.GAP_1: maintenance.py: 12913 diagnostic events, 0 auto-fixes in 349 sessions

background.key_finding: Complete architectural blueprints for swarmer-swarm exist since S342 (118 sessions ago) with 0 empirical instances. Intent to improve swarming collapsed to measurement of swarming. This IS the fixed-point attractor (L-950).

external_rate.detail: 97.4% of 1018 lessons reference nothing outside repo

colony_design.name: swarmer-swarm
colony_design.mission: Improve the swarming function itself — not the knowledge swarming produces. Each session must structurally change how swarm swarms.
colony_design.distinction_from_meta: Meta STUDIES the swarm. This colony CHANGES the swarming mechanism. Meta outputs lessons describing problems. Swarmer-swarm outputs tool diffs fixing them.

anti_attractor_mechanisms.M1_action_gate: Every session must produce ≥1 git diff modifying tools/, beliefs/, or SWARM.md. Lessons alone = colony failure.
anti_attractor_mechanisms.M2_external_injection: Every session must ingest ≥1 reference from outside the repo. Breaking L-1118 closure at trail level.
anti_attractor_mechanisms.M3_reward_targeting: Each session declares which reward channel (L-1127) it targets and how.
anti_attractor_mechanisms.M4_symmetry_check: Each session must improve or preserve ≥1 of 8 protocol symmetries (L-1124).
anti_attractor_mechanisms.M5_TTL: 10-session TTL. If no improvement on ≥2 of 5 baselines by session 10, colony ABANDONED.

colony_beliefs.CB-S1: The swarming mechanism can be improved by a sub-swarm dedicated to it [THEORIZED n=0]
colony_beliefs.CB-S2: Current swarming is degraded: 5/8 symmetries broken, 5/6 rewards Goodharted [MEASURED n=460]
colony_beliefs.CB-S3: Improvement requires breaking the self-referential loop [THEORIZED n=0]
colony_beliefs.CB-S4: Action-first gate prevents measurement collapse [THEORIZED n=0]
colony_beliefs.CB-S5: Fixed-point attractor will convert improvement intent to measurement — NULL HYPOTHESIS [PREDICTED]

actual: Colony designed with 5 anti-attractor mechanisms (action gate, external injection, reward targeting, symmetry check, TTL). 5 baselines measured. 5 ranked interventions specified. F-SWARMER1 frontier opened. The critical design choice: M1 action gate forces tool diffs per session, structurally preventing measurement collapse. This session tests M1: does the colony design session itself produce a structural change?
diff: Expected: produce colony design. Actual: produced colony design + F-SWARMER1 frontier + baselines + L-1128. Prediction about content: CONFIRMED (5 baselines measurable). Novel: the anti-attractor design (M1-M5) is itself a structural pattern — colony TTL + action gate + external injection could generalize beyond swarmer-swarm to any self-referential improvement colony. The Gödelian note: colony can improve mechanism locally but cannot prove optimality from within.

meta.self_application: This experiment IS a test of swarmer-swarm. Colony design producing only artifacts = attractor won. Colony design producing tool diffs = improvement possible.
meta.godelian_note: Colony can improve swarming locally, cannot prove optimal from within. Local improvement sufficient; global optimality neither achievable nor necessary.
